html:
Napatech A/S (OSL:NAPA): The Small FPGA Networking Name That Finally Has Proof

Napatech is not the $75 million revenue business described in some stale theses. It is much smaller and much more option-like. FY2025 revenue was only DKK 146.6 million, or USD 22.4 million, and 2026 guidance is DKK 200-240 million, or roughly USD 32-38 million. At NOK 49.00 per share, 110.2 million shares, and about NOK 5.4 billion market cap, the stock is optically expensive at roughly 15x 2026 revenue net of cash. That is the bear argument.

The variant view is that this is the wrong denominator. Napatech is crossing from a low-volume programmable SmartNIC supplier into production AI infrastructure. The May 8, 2026 first AI infrastructure production order is the first real proof point: 1,000 units, multi-million-dollar value, for a next-generation platform used in AI inference systems, tied to a 2025 design win and described by the company as the start of a multi-year deployment. The original AI design-win release said the program could exceed $15 million per year at peak production. One customer can become almost half of 2026 guided revenue.

That is the whole setup. A NOK 5.4 billion market cap looks absurd against DKK 220 million of near-term revenue, but much less absurd if 2026 is the first production year of a new AI networking product cycle and Napatech’s 27 unique 2025 design wins begin converting. The company’s Q1 2026 deck showed 5 new Q1 design wins, a fintech design win with more than $3 million lifetime opportunity and $1.5 million expected in 2026, the AI inference engagement moving toward production, and a tier-1 server OEM progressing through qualification. This is no longer only a slide-deck pivot.

The market still wants to categorize Napatech as a niche telco/5G offload vendor. That misses why FPGA-based programmable NICs matter now. AI inference clusters are not just GPU boxes. They are distributed systems whose economic output depends on deterministic, low-latency, high-throughput data movement. Napatech’s N3070X and F3070X products are built around Altera Agilex FPGAs and Intel Xeon D processing, aiming at 400G acceleration, AI pipelines, storage, cybersecurity, and cloud-native workloads. This is not a generic NIC card story. It is a bet that programmable network acceleration becomes a required layer where standards, model architectures, and customer hardware stacks keep changing.

NVIDIA is the obvious competitive threat. BlueField-4 supports 800 Gb/s throughput and NVIDIA is bundling Spectrum-X, BlueField, ConnectX, DOCA, and full-stack AI factory architecture. For hyperscalers standardizing fully on NVIDIA, Napatech may be irrelevant. But that is not the entire market. The opportunity is in non-NVIDIA or mixed inference stacks, AI ASIC vendors, fintech latency systems, storage acceleration, DLP/security acceleration, telecom edge, and server OEM programs where customers want Ethernet, programmability, deterministic performance, and supplier flexibility without adopting a full NVIDIA software and hardware stack.

The d-Matrix disclosure is important because it shows the type of customer Napatech can win: AI inference ASIC platforms that need efficient scale-out connectivity across servers. The May 2026 order customer was not named, but the language is consistent with that class of design win. The Broadcom Symantec DLP collaboration shows another path: using Napatech’s programmable NICs to accelerate high-throughput security workloads without crushing CPU performance. These are not all hyperscaler moonshots. They are repeatable infrastructure pain points.

Financially, Q1 2026 was encouraging but not yet decisive. Revenue rose 53% in DKK to DKK 36.6 million, gross margin stayed near 70%, EBITDA improved to negative DKK 18.1 million from negative DKK 29.1 million, and free cash flow improved to negative DKK 5.8 million from negative DKK 29.4 million. Cash and equivalents were DKK 99.1 million, with DKK 120.6 million available including unused credit facilities. Management held 2026 guidance unchanged after the AI order, with gross margin guided to 60-70% because AI infrastructure products carry lower margin than the legacy/core mix.

That lower AI gross margin is not a problem if volume arrives. The model has operating torque. At DKK 220 million of 2026 revenue, Napatech still loses money. At DKK 500-700 million of revenue, with 60-65% gross margin and opex growing far slower than sales, EBITDA can move into the DKK 100-200 million range. If several design wins mature into multi-year programs, current EV is not 15x sales, it is 15-30x plausible 2028 EBITDA for a still-growing AI infrastructure component supplier. That is where the forward valuation starts to look cheap.

The retail and market-structure setup adds torque. Top 20 holders own about 70% of the company, Investorprat shows roughly 2,683 Nordnet holders and 156 Avanza holders, and the stock is now being discussed in Norway as an “AI rocket” after the first production order. ABG’s visible NOK 40 target from early March is stale versus the post-order stock. This is exactly the kind of small Oslo name where price discovery can lag until liquidity suddenly appears, then overshoot once the market realizes the business is not just recovering, but changing category.

The key risks are real. Napatech could miss the ramp, component supply could constrain deliveries, NVIDIA or Broadcom could absorb the relevant use cases, AI customers could insource or redesign around standard NICs, and the company could remain too small to fund the support burden demanded by major OEMs. The recent warrant exercises also remind investors that dilution is part of the story, though the share count is now clearly measurable at about 110.2 million.

My base case is not that Napatech becomes the next NVIDIA-adjacent platform company. It is that core infrastructure stabilizes, the AI inference order scales, fintech and cybersecurity design wins convert, and revenue reaches roughly DKK 600 million by 2028 with positive EBITDA and a higher-quality backlog. That supports a NOK 90 target, or about 84% upside from NOK 49.00.

Bear case: AI design wins fail to scale, revenue remains around DKK 200-250 million, the stock derates from AI option value back to niche hardware economics. Target NOK 24, about 51% downside.

Base case: 2028 revenue around DKK 600 million, EBITDA positive, multiple still premium because design-win conversion remains active. Target NOK 90, about 84% upside.

Bull case: the first AI inference customer becomes a true multi-year volume program, several of the 27 2025 design wins convert, a tier-1 server OEM qualifies the platform, and Napatech is valued as a scarce programmable AI networking supplier or strategic acquisition target. Target NOK 180, about 267% upside.
